news.summary: On Thursday, Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) surpassed the New Taiwan Dollar to claim the eighth spot in terms of market capitalization. The cryptocurrency approached $112,000, maintaining its record-breaking streak. As a result, Bitcoin's market capitalization reached $2.20 trillion, outstripping the currencies of prominent economies. Data from AssetsMarketCap revealed that Bitcoin now outranks Taiwan's currency, as well as the Australian dollar and the Canadian dollar. This milestone solidifies Bitcoin's position as the world's most valuable cryptocurrency.
news.headline: Bitcoin's market capitalization reached $2.20 trillion, surpassing New Taiwan Dollar and becoming the eighth-largest currency globally.

news.summary: On May 23, 2025, in Singapore, the Worldwide Stablecoin Payment Network (WSPN) revealed the debut of its revamped website, showcasing the company's enhanced vision and prowess as a predominant figure in advanced stablecoin infrastructure. This redesign signifies a strategic move by WSPN to align with its ever-growing range of services and advancements in the field.
news.headline: WSPN launches new website in Singapore, showcasing enhanced vision in stablecoin infrastructure.

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, the 4th China-Central and Eastern European Countries (CEEC) Expo & International Consumer Goods Fair commenced in Ningbo, Zhejiang Province. The event, themed "New Visions for the Future," attracted considerable attention. This expo served as a platform for economic cooperation and cultural exchange amongst 17 CEEC nations and China. Notable figures include over 500 participating companies, with more than 1,000 products on display. This exhibition aims to strengthen trade ties and foster collaboration across various industries.
news.headline: 4th China-CEEC Expo & International Consumer Goods Fair started in Ningbo, China on May 22, 2025, focusing on "New Visions."

news.summary: The latest CNN Money Fear and Greed index indicated a slight uptick in market sentiment, remaining in the "Greed" zone. On Thursday, the U.S. stock market saw mixed results, with the Nasdaq Composite surging over 50 points. President Donald Trump's tax bill narrowly passed the House in a tight 215-214 vote and is now headed to the Senate for review. The Congressional Budget Office projects the bill could potentially increase the national deficit by around $3 trillion over the next ten years. Additionally, U.S. initial jobless claims dropped by 2,000 from the prior week to 227,000 for the week ending May 17, surpassing market expectations set at 230,000. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Nasdaq rises over 50 points after positive economic reports, fear index stays in 'Greed' zone. Trump's tax bill adds $3 trillion to deficit. Jobless claims decrease to 227,000.

news.summary: RedBird Capital Partners has recently revealed its acquisition agreement with The Telegraph Media Group for a total enterprise valuation of £500 million. This acquisition marks a significant milestone in the UK print media industry as the largest investment in this sector in the past ten years. By becoming the sole control owner of The Telegraph, RedBird aims to usher in a new phase of growth for the 170-year-old publication. As part of their growth strategy, focus will be on capital investment in the digital operations of the group to further enhance subscriber engagement and drive overall expansion.
news.headline: RedBird Capital Partners to acquire Telegraph Media Group for £500m, largest UK print media investment in a decade.

news.summary: President Trump's "One Big, Beautiful Bill Act" aims to reduce taxes, putting more money in people's pockets. The bill will eliminate taxes on social security benefits, tips, and overtime, benefiting many Americans. It also proposes a $40,000 state and local tax deduction. However, experts question if the tax cuts will favor value or growth stocks in the U.S. economy. The bill is estimated to add $3.8 trillion to the federal government's $36.2 trillion debt over the next decade. Louis Navellier suggests that increased consumer spending due to tax cuts will boost retail sales, potentially benefiting stocks like Brinker International Inc. and CAVA Group Inc. Retail sales at bars and restaurants rose by 1.2% in April. He also anticipates Federal Reserve interest rate cuts in the coming months.
news.headline: Trump's tax bill could boost economy, benefitting stocks like EAT & CAVA. Bill may add $3.8 trillion to $36.2 trillion debt.

news.summary: An international operation named Operation RapTor has resulted in the apprehension of 270 individuals and the confiscation of a total of $200 million in both cash and cryptocurrency.
news.headline: $200M seized from 270 suspects in global darknet operation, Operation RapTor.

news.summary: On May 23, 2025, in London, an international forum titled Singularity 3.0: The Convergence of AI, Web3, and Gaming was brought to a close. The event was co-hosted by Endless Web3 Genesis Cloud (Endless) and the Surrey Academy for Blockchain and Metaverse Applications (SABMA) at the University of Surrey. The conference brought together experts and enthusiasts to discuss the latest in artificial intelligence, Web3 technology, and gaming.
news.headline: Singularity 3.0 conference successfully wrapped up, showcasing AI, Web3, and Gaming convergence, hosting by Endless Web3 & SABMA at University of Surrey.

Tanmiah Food Company, also known as TFC and traded as 2281 on the Saudi Exchange, which has been operating since 1962, is at the forefront of these initiatives.
news.headline: Tanmiah Food Co. signs 2 MOUs at Saudi-U.S. Investment Forum to boost agri-food innovation; supports Vision 2030.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures are trading with a mix this morning on Friday. Wall Street is anticipating Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corp. (NYSE: BAH) to reveal earnings of $1.59 per share with revenue totaling $3.03 billion before the market opens. Booz Allen Hamilton's shares saw a 1.5% increase, reaching $131.10 in after-hours trading. On the other hand, Autodesk Inc. (NASDAQ: ADSK) announced better-than-expected financial results for the first quarter, provided robust guidance for the second quarter, and elevated its FY26 guidance. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: On Friday, U.S. stock futures were mixed. Booz Allen Hamilton expected to report $1.59/share on $3.03 billion revenue. Autodesk Inc. performing well.

news.summary: In Montreal, Mandatory and NuORDER by Lightspeed, top B2B commerce platforms are collaborating once again to support Mandatory's in-person trade event in Copenhagen from August 5-7. This partnership, now in its third year, highlights the growing trend of hybrid commerce, blending physical and digital interactions for enhanced buying experiences. This initiative aligns with the Nordic fashion industry's growth, showcasing the intersection of physical and digital commerce for increased flexibility and innovation.
news.headline: NuORDER by Lightspeed and Mandatory renew partnership for Copenhagen event promoting hybrid commerce.

news.summary: Frontline plc (NYSE: FRO) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on May 23. Analysts predict earnings of 23 cents per share, a decrease from 62 cents per share last year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $264.19 million, down from $556.03 million in the previous year. In the fourth quarter, the company saw a 6.9% revenue growth year-over-year to $443.491 million, beating the consensus of $300.67 million. Frontline's shares closed at $17.18, a 2.9% decrease. For more details and analyst ratings, visit the full article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Frontline plc (NYSE: FRO) to release Q1 earnings on May 23 with expected EPS of 23 cents, down from 62 cents YoY. Revenue forecasted at $264.19 million, a significant decrease from $556.03 million YoY. Q4 revenue grew by 6.9% YoY to $443.491 million. Frontline shares closed at $17.18, dropping by 2.9%.

news.summary: The $3.8 trillion tax-and-spending package proposed by President Donald Trump was narrowly approved by the U.S. House of Representatives with a slim margin of 215-214. The move caused a stir in bond markets, leading to a spike in 30-year Treasury yields to 5.15%, the highest level seen since October 2023. The bill, dubbed by Trump as his "big, beautiful" tax bill, is now on its way to the Senate for approval before the July 4 recess. While the package includes immediate economic stimulus measures such as widespread tax reductions and increased defense spending, experts are cautioning about potential long-term fiscal impacts. Jed Ellerbroek, a portfolio manager at Argent Capital Management, remarked on the bill's implications, noting that it lowers taxes for many and ramps up spending particularly in defense. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump's $3.8 trillion bill narrowly passed House by 215-214 margin. Experts expect short-term boost but warn of worsening deficits. Bond markets react with 30-year Treasury yields spiking to 5.15%, highest level since October 2023. Bill heads to Senate for approval before July 4 recess.税cuts, increased defense spending promised for short-term stimulus, but analysts warn of long-term fiscal consequences. Portfolio manager at Argent Capital Management states it reduces taxes for many & increases spending on defense.

news.summary: Hong Kong's premier university, HKUST, has extended a warm welcome to students from Harvard University. The invitation encourages international undergraduate students to transfer or enroll in degree programs at HKUST. This move showcases the university's dedication to fostering global academic exchange and collaboration. The announcement was made on May 23, 2025.
news.headline: HKUST invites Harvard students to transfer/enroll in its programs. Global academic shifts lead to opportunities.

news.summary: Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation, listed on NYSE as BAH, is set to reveal its fourth-quarter earnings on May 23. Analysts' predictions suggest earnings of $1.61 per share, showing growth from $1.33 per share in the same quarter last year. Based in McLean, Virginia, the company is expected to announce quarterly revenue of $3.03 billion, an increase from $2.77 billion in the previous year. Recently, Booz Allen Hamilton collaborated with Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META) to unveil "Space Llama," an AI solution aimed at improving maintenance and repair operations on the International Space Station. The closing stock price for Booz Allen Hamilton was $129.13, reflecting a 0.6% increase, as of last Thursday. For further details, readers can consult the latest analyst ratings on the Analyst section of a financial platform.
news.headline: Wall Street analysts raise Booz Allen Hamilton's Q4 earnings forecast to $1.61 per share, revenue $3.03 billion. Introduced AI solution "Space Llama" for ISS maintenance. Stock up 0.6% at $129.13.
